title_guess: France 1992: Loire & Périgord (Pont-Audemer, Chinon, Rocamadour, Conques)

pages.text:
We stopped in at a camping stop near Lichfield. There were several electric power stations on the River Trent, each with a hill of coal brought by barge from the Yorkshire & Nottinghamshire coal fields.

July 19th Sunday
	We all left at 9.30. for Portsmouth then had to go back home from Selly Oak for Simon to switch off Thomas' keyboard & other electrics.
	We had lunch in the queues on the quayside, & had to keep interrupting to move up. On the boat we paid to see Hook, then had a delicious, eat-as-much-as-you-want meal by P&O for £50.
	Once off the boat we motored for ½ - 1 hr. to a small campsite in a village, ending up in the dark by mistake in the residential end. ** Pont-Audemer

July 20th
	We set off & found a prettier campsite

in a field for our way back. We'll send the address to Laura, & her father might drive her there to join us the day before the ferry leaves.

	We motored a lot of the day & the boys fought furiously. We had lunch at Abbaye de la Trappe a monastery.

	In the evening we arrived at __Ruilly__ ****. A lovely, cool river to swim in. V. humid. Clean loos. William & Peter weren't allowed to swim. We had a good anniversary meal at St. Pierre, along to road in the village. No choice, just plonked in front of you, so much easier with the children. John had pooed twice on the ferry, & Charles x3, so we're trying to cut down on their food.

	In the night there was a fabulous thunderstorm. It looked like the sky was going to be blasted, lifted right of.

Simon went out to check William & John in the tent, getting soaked. Then Eve sent him out again to be in the tent during the storm. A branch broke off the willow tree above us, but fortunately got caught & didn't fall.

	Many trees were snapped off in the night. We are driving through a very fertile countryside, apples, caves, beautiful, honey-coloured buildings, & Cotswolds, v. soft stone, caves. We cross the R. Loire on a long suspension bridge, supported on 4 towers. Charles screams when he has to leave the front seat. He has not yet understood the rota system. He's using words like "wide" river, "actually".

__Tuesday July 21st__.

	We drove to Azay-le-Rideau William & the other boys liked the tapestry of the judgement of Solomon. There is a lovely facade & bas relief, light & feathery.

pages.sketch_desc: Small hand-drawn sketch at the bottom of the page depicting a Mississippi-style riverboat with paddle wheel, funnels and a flag.
pages.text:
Afterwards we had crêpes.
	Then we drove on for 2 nights at __Richilieu__ ***
	__Wednesday July 22nd.__
	We went out towards Chinon. On the way we passed a beautiful old chapel __Champigny sur Veude__ 1499 lovely. proportions Sainte Chapelle, Stained glass windows, + marble statue of kneeling duke.
	On to Chinon. We parked at the top of the ridge + walked down into the old town with narrow streets. We saw a wine-making/coopering museum, animated models + English recorded commentary
	We had a good chicken lunch, but it was rather slow, so we went just down the road for icecreams. Chinon had a superb-looking Mississippi riverboat

In the afternoon we climbed the hill back to the top to look at the long castle. There was a loo in the castle, + a huge, flat space at the top with trees growing. Several towers: in one the tower was a puppet show — very very good. One man puppeteer on a wagon in a room. Blue celophane shook as the sea, a sleeping monk fell out of a sail boat + drowned + his soul was carried up to God by a knight on a winged horse. We saw a king + queen watching court musicians + a juggler + a tournament. Joan of Arc recognised the Dauphin among his courtiers. Finally a giant — 2 legs — fell asleep with his feet over the castle walls.
	We drove back to Richilieu + swam. Eve + William washed clothes.
	__Thu July 23rd__
	We walked around Richilieu — lovely city walls, still lived in in higgledy piggledy houses + lush gardens in the old moat. There's a beautiful covered market.

At 12. we set off south & lunch in a beautiful spot by a river
William & Thomas fought & William was kicked in the thigh & had a mega tantrum.
	Too hot in the afternoon, after checking 2 campsites on the way we finally got to __St. Estephe__ ** & swam. It was rather crowded, & the loos were poorly lit & not clean in the evening especially. There was a lake, but we had to motorrow it to reach the beach.

__Fri July 23rd.__
	It rained as we ~~set~~ got up, so we sat in the van, reading. We went to Piégut to shop, & returned to the lake beach to make lunch. In the afternoon we swam & hired pedaloes. We spent a couple of hours in the camp & then went out to a restaurant in Piégut - the boys had pigeon & we, Simon & I, had duck.

We usually order 5 helpings & divide them up among us. ~~When we~~ We started supper at [?10] 8.05. & left about 9.45. The boys were good & in bed by 10.30.

__Sat July 24th__
	The day looked brighter. We set off south & stopped at __Brantôme__. Monks had founded a monastery in the cliffs with a special spring. They eventually built an L shaped bridge to reach the village. The church had a lovely [?C] bas relief, small, over the font, of smiling figures - St. John Baptising Jesus, Jesus' robe held by an angel. We looked at the ruins of the cliffs.
	The town is beautiful today - balustraded walks along the wide river, trout old houses. The monks had left behind a tall bell tower with large [?L] gables against the triangular roof.
	We had lunch in an overgrown meadow next to the river. Then we drove on

to the cave of __Rouffignac__. We bookd tickets on a train half-an-hour later, & dashed back up the hill to the van for a cup of tea & patisserie.

    The caves were wonderful, 'grabbing', scraped drawings of mammoths, or black charcoal. Also modern grafiti as old as 1760. Sea caves. We had a good guide. The trip took an hour 5.15-6.15, on the way back Charles was bursting for a pee, so Thomas & I had to help him lean perilously off the train for a [?].

    We looked at 3 campsites near __Les Eyzies__, zigzagging round the hills, before finding a lovely farm "Le Queylou" ★★★★  run by a Dutchman. A superb view of green hillside & valley & one red-tiled roof. Modern facilities at one end of an old barn. At the other end were picnic tables & a ping pong table.

__Sunday July 25__

    We set off quite late & got to church at __Tayac__ 20 minutes before the service at 11.00. The best church we'd been to in France. Full of people at all ages. Enthusiasm, awareness. The church is partly carved into, built into the hillside. Simon & Thomas stayed in [illegible] until the end of the sermon. Eve, as usual, went out after 20 minutes with the rest of the children. The priest spoke about needing to come to God & sunbatte in prayer.

    We had a good lunch in __Les Eyzies__ at the __Vieux Moulin__. There was a water wheel turning cogs inside the restaurant. Simon & Eve had the cheapest meal ~~£50~~ 85 francs each, the boys had 50 francs each. Simon got so fed up with Peter's & William's fight after church that he threatend to

leave them in residential summer camp for 3 weeks. They stopped fighting from 11.45 until 8. pm.
	After lunch we walked along the road to the Caves of the __Font de Gaume__, but there was a 3 day wait to get in. They only allow 375 people per day inside, & they've the best caves after Lascaux, which are now closed. We went on a 6 mile drive to a very crowded lake. Simon didn't like the look of what was floating in the water. Charles waded knee-deep out to a slide & shot down it into deep water! He did look indignant when his ring brought him up to the surface. John swims with one arm-band, so that I can see him.
	We returned to Les Eyzies to see the caving museum- Scrapers, statues

captions: Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket.
pages.text:
& skeletons.
	Back at the camp we had spaghetti for supper, because we'd been unable to buy bread. It was a beautiful evening.
	~~We~~ __Monday July 26th__
	We set off & stopped at __St. Cyprien__ to shop. (delicious [?boulang-] not selling pastries)

Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket.

Thomas is doing all the map reading, very well! We went to Daglan, Nr. Domme to see Monique, Eve's old tennis partner, but she was on holiday in Spain. We met M. Fayolle, her mother, very nice. She suggested that we go to Rocamadour to see the eagles.

      We had lunch in the middle of a small village, Fon[?], next to the stream. Then we drove on to __Rocamadour__. We caught a slanting lift down to see the old shrine. There is a black madonna, enthroned, with a regal child on her lap. Simon thought it a very beautiful statue, slightly off-centre. Hanging from the ceiling is a bell which rings when a miracle is about to happen. Both the statue & the bell are very old & date from before the first of the sackings of Rocamadour in about (13. Warring lords kept sacking Rocamadour because of all its pilgrim riches, including Plantagenet princes: these culminated in the protestants' sacking it & destroying

much of it. It was only revived as a pilgrimage centre 100 years ago.
	Soon after leaving Rocamadour we passed a campsite with swimming pool in a pretty field, & decided to stay there for the night. **/** "Camping le lot"
It had a small swimming pool & a lovely old stone barn. Other features which attracted the boys were the pingpong table, breadcroissant, badminton, playgrounds.
	In the morning we returned to __Rocamadour__ to watch the eagles. They have a remarkable birds sanctuary — everything from black kites which eat grasshoppers, vultures, owls, & eagles. The head man — a power maniac; stood us out in the hot sun & harangued us for ages on the ideals of his zoo — saving birds, pronature antipollution.... Finally the birds started flying. They were remarkable — they come when one of

the keepers raises his gloved arm. One fish-eater, skimmed across a pool to scoop up a fish. Others swooped in front of us from tower to tower. One bird was put on John's & Peter's heads. 2 [?cuts] eagles went off into the sky & refused to come back. They were still messing about when we left.
	On the way to __Casanourse__ **** we stopped to book in at Le Beau Sejour at Calvinet. They wanted us at 8 at the latest. People seem to eat earlier down here — there were already people eating when we walked in.
	When we reached the campsite we swam & Eve did a load of wash — the da 4 days of clothes, one load, for £2. Such a relief. The next day I put all the bed sheets & pyjamas in.
	At Calvinet we had our best meal yet. Eve had Charlotte-cold, subtle-tasting mold of cucumber & potato,

cold, sweet tomato & oil. Then I had poached egg on vinegared bread & goose liver cream. The main course was duckling in a dark brown sauce & a mould of round potatoes, crisp ones on top. For pud I had coffee, chocolates & caramel sauce.
	Back at the camp, Charles slept in the cab, Thomas outside, & William & Peter & John in the tent.

__Wednesday 28th July__
	We spent the day in camp. The bread van brought breakfast at 9.15. We swam & had a pedalo fight. Usually we have salami for breakfast, but we didn't that day because of such an enormous supper. We had salami & salad & plums for lunch. For tea we had rockcakes & for supper, cheese, tomato & yog.
	Thomas & John slept out, but Eve felt a drop of rain (was it rain?) in the middle of the night, & insisted on taking

Thomas & John into the tent. There were 14 cyclists, all on purple bikes, just down the hill, about 18 years old, having a super outing sleeping under the stars.

__Thursday 29th July__
	Set off for __Entraques__. One of the nicest towns we've been in. Old, & not much done-up. A calm, well-kept church, an old wooden gate with a door-knocker – a hand tapping on a forehead. Old stone/wood buildings, with over-hanging upper storeys. We had lunch in a restaurant near the river. We used the bank & post office as well as buying food.
	On the way home we booked 2 canoes for 9.15 am. Saturday.
	Lovely patisseries for supper.
	John & William slept outside.

__Friday 30th July__
	As usual delicious croissants from 9.15 bread van.
	We're spending the day swimming &

pedaloing. Simon is trying to sort out pocket money, while Eve writes post cards. The boys have made friends with a family from Leicester, Steve & Pauline, Dan, Ben & [?Jen-] [?Swanton] at school at Stoneygates
__Sat 31st July__
	We left early & had breakfast at the canoe depot. Simon, Thomas & William & Peter went canoeing, while John & Charles & Eve waited. John & Charles paddled while Eve read. They returned in a foul temper because Peter had refused to paddle when he wasn't with Daddy.
	We had lunch at Vielleville, & then returned, to spend the afternoon at Casanrouse.
__Sumer 1st August__
	It would have been better to leave at 9.30, but we only left at 10. because of waiting for croissants

from the bread van. Then we set off for Conques. We parked down in the valley belt below the little chapel. Last year we tried to walk down to it, but it was too hot. This year we started to walk up at 10.55.. It was padlocked, but the walk was marvellous. Old, old, village, steep pedestrian streets, water for horses to drink, central gutters down the streets, lush gardens. We arrived at the abbey at 11.10. Mass had started already. It was very cool at the back.
	We bought leather & wooden toys & had a rather stuffy lunch with special ice creams. We sat outside, but it would have been cooler in the dark interior. After lunch we looked at the cloisters & cemetery. They have built an amazingly high wall to make a flat place for the cemetery. We [booked]

tickets for the Haydon, Bartok Quartets on Monday evening. The restaurant was the first one which had not allowed us to use access. We are now short of cash, & the banks are closed on Monday.

__Monday 2nd August__
	The campsite 'owner' telephoned her boss, who telephoned someone else, so that we can pay by Travellers cheques. The Casaniouge bank only opens on Wednesday morning.
	We have spent the day swimming & washing clothes, & trying to get things dry after a shower of rain.
	He swam again with the Swantons & left for Conques at 6.0. We looked around the town, had supper & left William, Charles & John in the square, on the stone bench outside the Abbey church. The Benedictine monks were friendly & [?chatted]. We left 2 sleeping bags &

several books. [?to] The concert was marvellous in the floodlit nave. The programme was Mozart, Bartok & Schumann. Very beautiful.
	Afterwards we woke Peter up off Simon's lap & went & collect John & Charles, who were both asleep, & William. Simon carried Charles back to the van & strapped him onto the kitchen seat. John was very good & walked back holding Eve's hand & a piggy-back half-way. We drove about 3 miles north from Conques & turned off into a field & all slept in the van [?2.00] John slept on the floor & Eve could just use the loo in the night. There was a fantastic view on both sides from the field.

__Tues [?August]__
	We had breakfast in the van - the boys complained it was cold.

pages.sketch_desc: Outline map of France with a route line drawn from le thore / Vallée de la Seine in the north, down through Châteaux de la Loire and Bourges, then south-west to Périgord Caves, and south to Gorges de Truyères, Cassaniouze, and Conques. Year label '1992' appears to the right of the map.
pages.text:
We drove along winding roads through beautiful little villages, & arrived at Etraques, where it's market day, to shop. Then on up the valley of the Truyères, with hydro electric dams, & purpose-built 1930's villages.

[Route sketch of France, labelled 1992, with route line showing:] le thore / Vallée de la Seine / Châteaux de la Loire / Bourges / Périgord Caves / Gorges de Truyères / Cassaniouze / Conques

We had lunch in a very dirty wood & came on to a crowded, but pleasant campsite on the La Source* * * Lac du Sarrans, created by the Sarrans

pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn diagram of the swimming pool layout at the campsite, showing: chute on the left leading into the pool, 'deep' and 'swimming' sections, a bridge above, sun-warmed seat at top left, foot bath at top right, a 'shoe' area, '4 sprays into pool' entering from the right, 'steps down', and a 'chil's pool+slide' at bottom left. Also annotated 'eating' area outside the pool. Caption reads 'Pool in confined area at campsite.'
pages.text:
damn. There's a swimming pool where we all enjoyed swimming, & John grew more & more confident swimming out from the chute. We all had an ice cream afterwards to celebrate his swimming the width.

[Sketch of pool layout at campsite, labelled:] sun-warmed seat / bridge / foot bath / chute / deep / swimming / shoe / 4 sprays into pool / steps down / chil's pool+slide

Pool in confined area at campsite.     eating

Wash-rooms under tennis court.

__Wed August 5th__

      We wanted to leave early, but stayed for a swim. The pool only opened at 10.00.. We drove north [past] out of the Gorges & the Chestnut Trees. We spent hours on the A71, going past villages, castles & farm houses. We drove for 8 hours & ended up camping in quite a pretty campsite in Salbris * * *. The restaurant,

Hotel du Parc, was the best, most friendly of the holiday. I had pâté, fish (lieu-noir?) fromage-frais with chives + chocolate mousse pudding. The sign on paying in the lobby stated how much it was per personne, deux personne + chien R.C. 50fr. Anglian dogs obviously don't cross the channel.

__Thu August 6th__
		We chatted to a couple from London in the next tent who had been to the Olympics in Barcelona. They complained that ticket purchasing was not centralised.
		We set off towards Chartres + arrived at 12.50. Thomas + Eve spent an hour looking at stained-glass windows starting to devise a quiz for the B'ham cathedral choir trip in October.
		We then paid £13 for a snack lunch in a bar - jambon et fromage croissants, icecream + coffee.

After another 1½ hours' motoring we reached __St. Illiers la Ville__ ****+
jumped into the swimming pool which is perhaps rather heavily chlorinated.
(See P.80.).
		There is also a large trampoline which Peter particularly enjoys. Ten or so children sit around the edge of it, while each child in turn has a bounce in the middle, or else 5 or so children bounce up + down all at once. A P.E. instructor's nightmare. The trampoline is in a wood under tall trees in the shade, along with 2 ping pong tables.
		The site is only 5 minutes from the station, + the train run into Paris is 50 minutes, so it would be possible to do day trips to Paris or ~~De~~ Eurodisney from the site.
__Fri 6th August__
		We lazed about at the campsite enjoying the trampoline + swimming,

had lunch, + then set off to shop in Brownie + at a supermarket. We arrived at the campsite at 5.30. Laura + her father + Benjamin had already been here an hour, because we had said that we would arrive at 4. It is peaceful with an orchard + ping pong. Quite low key + old showers, but clean + friendly.
	Eve beat everyone at ping pong, except Thomas, who beat Eve 22-20.

__Sat 7th Aug__
	We got up at 5.3[0]15. + left at 6.15, without breakfast. We ate breakfast while mincing along in the traffic jams. On the boat we just had sandwiches + watched Sean Connery in "Medicine Man".
	After Southampton we called in on Hathie, but unfortunately they were all out. There were awful traffic jams. Finally we stopped + had tea in an almost
